message:
fix(fcp-webhook): deprecate racy check_replay + record_event pair (br-v3wrz)

Closes flywheel_connectors-v3wrz (P1/MEDIUM).

Reviewer (cod_3 P5) flagged that WebhookHandler exposed both check_replay()
and record_event() as public methods. Because the two are non-atomic, two
concurrent deliveries of the same event_id can both pass check_replay()
before either records — then both are processed, defeating the exactly-
once guarantee on untrusted webhook input. claim_event() (handler.rs:230)
already does the atomic check-and-insert under a single write lock, but
the split pair was still public and invited the TOCTOU race.

Fix: mark both methods #[deprecated(since = "0.1.0", note = "...")] and
steer callers to claim_event via docstrings on all three methods. No
production code uses the split pair — all call sites are tests —
confirmed via grep -rn 'handler\.(check_replay|record_event)'. Per
AGENTS.md no-backcompat policy, outright removal was considered; kept
as deprecated with clear note to preserve the regression surface (the
inline tests in handler.rs and the two integration test files that
specifically document the racy semantics are still the clearest prose
of why claim_event exists).

Added #[allow(deprecated)] at module scope on:
- crates/fcp-webhook/src/handler.rs::tests (inline)
- crates/fcp-webhook/tests/no_mock_integration.rs (16 callers)
- crates/fcp-webhook/tests/webhook_receiver_compliance.rs (3 callers)

Added regression test claim_event_is_atomic_under_concurrent_deliveries
that spawns 16 OS threads racing to claim_event(same_id) and asserts
exactly one Ok + THREADS-1 ReplayDetected via AtomicUsize counters. This
pins the property that motivated the deprecation and would fail if
claim_event ever regressed to the racy split pattern.

Also strengthened claim_event's docstring to name it as the only correct
way to enforce exactly-once on untrusted webhook input.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-webhook — 574/574 pass (466 lib + 102
integration + 6 no_mock, one more than the previous 573 baseline from
br-r3ygj thanks to the atomicity regression). cargo check -p fcp-webhook
--all-targets emits zero v3wrz deprecation warnings (only the unrelated
zp1xx/constraints_cbor one from fcp-core).

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose

files.patch:
@@ -3,6 +3,11 @@
 //! These tests exercise the full webhook pipeline (signature verification,
 //! event parsing, routing, dead letter queue) without any external mocks.
 
+// Pre-existing tests cover the deprecated check_replay + record_event pair
+// (br-v3wrz). Claim_event is the atomic replacement; see
+// claim_event_rejects_duplicate_under_split_call_pattern for the canonical
+// regression. Keep the legacy tests running under allow(deprecated).
+#![allow(deprecated)]
 #![allow(
     clippy::cast_possible_truncation,
     clippy::cast_precision_loss,
